scenario: A long onboarding form has 30+ fields organised into logical groups: Personal Info, Employment, Banking, Tax Details. The user is overwhelmed seeing all fields at once. The UX team wants a wizard-style multi-page flow where the user fills one section, clicks "Next", proceeds to the next section.
question: Which Camunda 8 Forms approach implements this multi-step wizard?

options.text: Split the form into multiple User Tasks, each with its own focused form covering one logical section (Personal Info, Employment, Banking, Tax Details). The BPMN process chains the User Tasks sequentially with Sequence Flow. Variables submitted at each step accumulate in process scope as the user progresses through the wizard. This is the canonical Camunda 8 wizard pattern: one User Task per wizard step, the BPMN orchestrates the multi-step flow, and each form stays focused on a manageable number of fields.

Use a single form containing a built-in Wizard component that organises the 30+ fields into sections with Next and Previous navigation buttons, submitting all data at once when the user reaches the final page. Camunda Forms does not include a Wizard component with Next/Previous pagination in its element library. The Forms component catalog provides input elements, layout containers (groups, columns), and display elements, but no multi-page wizard with built-in navigation. This is an invented feature.

Use Group components within a single form to visually separate the 30+ fields into labelled sections on the same page. The user sees all groups simultaneously without any Next/Previous navigation. Groups provide visual organization and collapsible sections in some contexts, but they do not paginate the form or hide earlier sections. All fields remain simultaneously visible, which directly contradicts the UX requirement to reduce overwhelm by showing one section at a time in a wizard-style flow.

Camunda Forms does not support multi-page flows natively; redirect users to an external multi-page web application where the form is rendered, then call back to the Camunda process after the user completes all sections. While external form applications can integrate with Camunda 8 via the task completion API, this adds significant implementation complexity, introduces a separate web application to build and maintain, and bypasses the native wizard pattern that multiple User Tasks with sequential BPMN flow provides out of the box.

a.text: Correct. Camunda 8 Forms are designed as single-step forms per User Task. To create a wizard-style multi-page flow, model the BPMN with multiple User Tasks chained sequentially. Each User Task has its own focused form. After the user completes section N, the process advances to User Task N+1; variables accumulate as the user progresses. This is the canonical wizard pattern in Camunda 8.

b.text: Incorrect. No "Wizard component" with Next/Previous in Camunda Forms.

c.text: Incorrect. Group components separate visually but don't paginate -- overwhelming for 30+ fields.

d.text: Incorrect. External app integration is heavier than the canonical multi-User-Task pattern.

explanation: Camunda 8 Forms are designed as single-step forms per User Task. To create a wizard-style multi-page flow, model the BPMN with multiple User Tasks chained sequentially. Each User Task has its own focused form. After the user completes section N, the process advances to User Task N+1; variables accumulate as the user progresses. This is the canonical wizard pattern in Camunda 8.
